tool_args.label: Student OMNY Program
tool_args.raw_text: › In 2024, MTA and NYCPS rolled out Student OMNY with 670K cards across 2600 schools › Usage grew by 35% › Fare evasion on the bus fell by 4pp › The MTA Data Lake tracked this program in real-time › Cards not being used appropriately could be identified and deactivated

payload.description: In 2024, MTA and NYCPS rolled out Student OMNY with 670K cards across 2600 schools. Usage grew by 35%. Fare evasion on the bus fell by 4pp. The MTA Data Lake tracked this program in real-time.

tool_args.label: Availability declined after COVID
tool_args.raw_text: Availability had been declining from 2016 to 2019 – but dropped 10 days after COVID, driven by increased sick leave and Workers Comp (WC)

payload.claim_text: Availability had been declining from 2016 to 2019 – but dropped 10 days after COVID, driven by increased sick leave and Workers Comp (WC)
payload.subject: MTA employee availability

tool_args.label: Data Lake changing supply chain transparency
tool_args.raw_text: The Data Lake and the team at Data and Analytics have been instrumental in bringing more transparency into the MTA's supply chain across Operating Agencies. The initiative allowed us to take disparate systems responsible for forecasting and inventory management and align on key metrics systematically to help drive improvements.

payload.claim_text: The Data Lake and the team at Data and Analytics have been instrumental in bringing more transparency into the MTA's supply chain across Operating Agencies
payload.subject: MTA Data Lake

warnings: claim.source is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Source/program names are relation context, not claim type. Suggested relation: data_provided_by or part_of_program.

tool_args.label: Data Lake used for OMNY go-to-market strategy
tool_args.raw_text: We built the OMNY go-to-market strategy on the MTA data lake. Access to data about payment method, rider class (student, Reduced Fare, Fair Fares, etc.), and station or bus route helped us develop, measure, and evolve the plan to help all customers make the switch to tap-and-ride before turning off MetroCard sales.

payload.claim_text: We built the OMNY go-to-market strategy on the MTA data lake. Access to data about payment method, rider class (student, Reduced Fare, Fair Fares, etc.), and station or bus route helped us develop, measure, and evolve the plan to help all customers make the switch to tap-and-ride before turning off MetroCard sales.
payload.subject: MTA Data Lake
payload.source: Jessica Lazarus – Deputy Chief, Commercial Ventures

warnings: claim.source is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Source/program names are relation context, not claim type. Suggested relation: data_provided_by or part_of_program.

tool_args.label: Customer complaints about missed transfers at Jamaica
tool_args.raw_text: › In early 2023, we began hearing complaints from customers about missed transfers at Jamaica › This was also apparent in our customer satisfaction surveys › Using data from the TrainTime app, we worked with LIRR to develop a "Jamaica on-time performance" metric

payload.claim_text: In early 2023, we began hearing complaints from customers about missed transfers at Jamaica. This was also apparent in our customer satisfaction surveys. Using data from the TrainTime app, we worked with LIRR to develop a 'Jamaica on-time performance' metric
payload.subject: LIRR Jamaica Station

tool_args.label: LIRR actions to improve Jamaica transfer experience
tool_args.raw_text: › Reallocated running times to better match typical operating conditions › Refined stopping patterns and routings to enable parallel moves › Focused on getting trains out of their origins on time › Ensured TrainTime shows multiple transfer opportunities and platform assignments earlier

payload.claim_text: Key LIRR actions: Reallocated running times to better match typical operating conditions, refined stopping patterns and routings to enable parallel moves, focused on getting trains out of their origins on time, ensured TrainTime shows multiple transfer opportunities and platform assignments earlier
payload.subject: Jamaica Station LIRR transfer experience

tool_args.label: CBTC data in MTA Data Lake
tool_args.raw_text: CBTC systems produce an immense wealth of data — billions of data points every week. Ingesting it into the MTA Data Lake allows us to perform large-scale data analysis to dive deep into train performance and system operation. It also enables us to fuse this with other sources – like EAM and incident reporting.

payload.claim_text: CBTC systems produce an immense wealth of data — billions of data points every week. Ingesting it into the MTA Data Lake allows us to perform large-scale data analysis to dive deep into train performance and system operation. It also enables us to fuse this with other sources – like EAM and incident reporting.
payload.subject: MTA Data Lake
payload.source: Kurt Raschke – Senior Director, Subway Systems
